pr.title: Cleanup of some duplicated data pointers and convert some hex to ASCII
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleaned asm/data.s by adding missing labels for addressable data inside large data blocks, replacing many raw absolute address constants with symbolic label references, and converting several hex-encoded ASCII strings into readable .asciz literals with alignment. The PR was a data-assembly readability and maintainability cleanup rather than a C decompilation change.

key_files.role: only changed file; converted raw data pointers and some hex-encoded strings to symbolic/readable assembly forms

what_changed: Added .global labels at interior data offsets such as lbl_803BD430 through lbl_803BDBC0 so repeated pointer-table entries could refer to symbols instead of hard-coded addresses.
Introduced a label for a large pointer table, lbl_803BDC18, and replaced long repeated runs of raw .4byte 0x803BD3D8-style constants with .4byte lbl_803BD3D8-style symbolic references.

Converted several big-endian hex word strings into .asciz strings followed by .balign 4, including "PlyMario5KGr_Share_joint", "PlyMario5KGr_Share_matanim_joint", "PlMrAJ.dat", "ftDemoResultMotionFileMario", and "ftDemoIntroMotionFileMario".

Replaced additional raw data-pointer constants with labels in later tables, including references to lbl_803DD6A0, lbl_803DD9A0, lbl_803DDC58, lbl_803DE1B8, lbl_803DE930, lbl_803DD6D0, and lbl_803DD888.
Added labels around jump/table-adjacent data such as lbl_803DD69C, lbl_803DD6A0, lbl_803DD6D0, lbl_803DD888, and lbl_803DD9A0.
Converted UI/test text from hex words to .asciz strings, including "<RESULT TEST>", "1P KIND :", "2P KIND :", "< Sugano Test >", and "STAFFROLL START >".
Replaced some string/data pointer slots with symbolic labels, for example raw 0x803FD78C, 0x803FD79C, 0x803FD7A8, and 0x803FDB78 became lbl_803FD78C, lbl_803FD79C, lbl_803FD7A8, and lbl_803FDB78.
Added labels for ToyFigure-related string/data offsets such as lbl_803FDF64 and lbl_803FDF84 and used those labels in repeated pointer entries.
smart_moves: Converted raw absolute addresses to labels only after adding matching .global label definitions at the pointed-to offsets, preserving the binary meaning while making the pointer relationships explicit.
Kept repeated pointer-table patterns intact while changing only their representation from numeric addresses to symbols, which reduces risk in a large asm/data.s cleanup.
Used .asciz for recognized null-terminated ASCII strings and followed them with .balign 4, preserving 4-byte alignment expected by the surrounding .4byte data.
Focused the PR narrowly on assembly data representation cleanup in a single file, making a large mechanical diff easier to reason about despite the high line count.
decomp_lessons: When a .4byte value is clearly an address into asm/data.s, prefer defining a label at that target and referencing the label instead of leaving a raw 0x80xxxxxx constant.
Repeated raw address constants in pointer tables are often a sign that missing labels can improve future struct/table recovery without changing layout.
Hex-encoded string words should be converted to .asciz only when the ASCII interpretation and null termination are clear; retain .balign 4 afterward when the surrounding data is word-aligned.
Adding labels at interior offsets can reveal implicit table element boundaries and make later C struct declarations or data-splitting work easier.
Data cleanup PRs can matter for decompilation even without matching a function, because symbolic pointers and readable strings provide evidence for future type and asset-name recovery.
naming_conventions: Used existing address-derived placeholder labels of the form lbl_803BD430, lbl_803BDC18, lbl_803DD6A0, and lbl_803FD78C.
Kept labels global with a preceding .global directive when they are referenced from pointer tables.
Did not introduce semantic symbol names for the newly labeled data; the cleanup stayed within the project’s lbl_ADDRESS convention for unknown data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace `.4byte 0x803xxxxx` with `.4byte lbl_803xxxxx` after defining `lbl_803xxxxx:` at the corresponding address.
For string literals represented as big-endian word hex, convert to `.asciz "..."` and add `.balign 4` to maintain subsequent data alignment.
Preserve table entry order and duplicate references exactly when converting raw pointer constants to label references.
Use labels at table/data boundaries to allow the assembler to resolve references instead of hard-coding absolute addresses.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were captured for this PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace remaining raw 0x803xxxxx pointers in asm/data.s with labels.
Search for .asciz conversions in asm/data.s to identify other asset-name or UI-string cleanup patterns.
Inspect users of lbl_803BDC18 to infer the structure of the repeated pointer table.
Look for later decompilation work that consumes lbl_803DD6A0, lbl_803DD9A0, lbl_803FD78C, or related labels as typed data.
Check whether ToyFigure-related strings around lbl_803FDF64 and lbl_803FDF84 were later given semantic names.
